content: Did you know that time moves differently for your head and your feet? Or that a teaspoon of neutron star would weigh billions of tons? These ten physics facts will blow your mind.
summary: Did you know that time moves differently for your head and your feet? Or that a teaspoon of neutron star would weigh billions of tons? These ten physics facts will blow your mind.
title: 10 Mind-Blowing Physics Facts

content:
What if you could shrink down to the size of an atom? Atom the Mouse does exactly that — and discovers a world stranger than any fantasy. In the quantum realm, particles tunnel through walls, exist in two states at once, and are connected across vast distances. This mind-bending adventure makes quantum physics accessible and unforgettable.
What you’ll learn:
Atomic structure — protons, neutrons, electrons Quantum mechanics basics — superposition and uncertainty Wave-particle duality at the atomic scale Quantum entanglement and the future of quantum computing
summary:
What if you could shrink down to the size of an atom? Atom the Mouse does exactly that — and discovers a world stranger than any fantasy. In the quantum realm, particles tunnel through walls, exist in two states at once, and are connected across vast distances. This mind-bending adventure makes quantum physics accessible and unforgettable.

What you’ll learn:

  • Atomic structure — protons, neutrons, electrons
  • Quantum mechanics basics — superposition and uncertainty
  • Wave-particle duality at the atomic scale
  • Quantum entanglement and the future of quantum computing
title: Atom the Mouse and the World Inside Everything

content:
About This Simulation Place objects on a balance beam and see if you can predict which way it will tip. Explore how mass AND distance from the pivot point both matter for balance.
Key concepts: Torque, lever arm, rotational equilibrium, center of mass
summary:
About This Simulation

Place objects on a balance beam and see if you can predict which way it will tip. Explore how mass AND distance from the pivot point both matter for balance.

title: Balancing Act

content:
About This Simulation Grab batteries, resistors, light bulbs, and wires to build circuits. Use the ammeter and voltmeter to measure current and voltage. See what happens when you connect bulbs in series vs parallel.
Key concepts: Electric current, voltage, resistance, Ohm’s Law, series and parallel circuits
summary:
About This Simulation

Grab batteries, resistors, light bulbs, and wires to build circuits. Use the ammeter and voltmeter to measure current and voltage. See what happens when you connect bulbs in series vs parallel.

Key concepts: Electric current, voltage, resistance, Ohm’s Law, series and parallel circuits
title: Circuit Construction Kit

content:
Echo the Dolphin can “see” with sound — but how? In this underwater adventure, Echo and friends explore the physics of sound waves, from the vibrating strings of a guitar to the sonar pulses that map the ocean floor. Discover how sound travels, why we hear echoes, and how music is really just math in disguise.
What you’ll learn:
Sound as mechanical waves — frequency, amplitude, wavelength How sound travels through different materials Resonance, harmonics, and the physics of music Echolocation, sonar, and ultrasound technology
summary:
Echo the Dolphin can “see” with sound — but how? In this underwater adventure, Echo and friends explore the physics of sound waves, from the vibrating strings of a guitar to the sonar pulses that map the ocean floor. Discover how sound travels, why we hear echoes, and how music is really just math in disguise.

What you’ll learn:

  • Sound as mechanical waves — frequency, amplitude, wavelength
  • How sound travels through different materials
  • Resonance, harmonics, and the physics of music
  • Echolocation, sonar, and ultrasound technology
title: Echo the Dolphin and the Invisible Symphony

content:
About This Simulation Build a half-pipe and watch the skater zoom back and forth. Track the energy bar graph to see kinetic energy transform into potential energy and back again.
Key concepts: Kinetic energy, potential energy, conservation of energy, thermal energy from friction
summary:
About This Simulation

Build a half-pipe and watch the skater zoom back and forth. Track the energy bar graph to see kinetic energy transform into potential energy and back again.

content:
About This Simulation Explore the forces at work when you push against objects. Create an applied force and see how it makes objects move. Adjust friction and see how it changes the motion.
Key concepts: Net force, friction, mass, acceleration, Newton’s Laws
summary:
About This Simulation

Explore the forces at work when you push against objects. Create an applied force and see how it makes objects move. Adjust friction and see how it changes the motion.

content:
Why do things fall down and not up? Gigi the Giraffe has always wondered, and with Professor Hoot the owl as her guide, she’s about to find out. From Newton’s apple to Einstein’s curved spacetime, from black holes to astronaut adventures — this book takes young readers on a gravity-defying journey through one of nature’s most fundamental forces.
What you’ll learn:
Why things fall — the basics of gravitational attraction Newton’s Law of Universal Gravitation Einstein’s revolutionary idea: gravity as curved spacetime Orbits, black holes, and the future of space travel Featuring beloved characters Bounce the kangaroo, Milo the mouse, and Captain Koko the astronaut monkey!
summary:
Why do things fall down and not up? Gigi the Giraffe has always wondered, and with Professor Hoot the owl as her guide, she’s about to find out. From Newton’s apple to Einstein’s curved spacetime, from black holes to astronaut adventures — this book takes young readers on a gravity-defying journey through one of nature’s most fundamental forces.

What you’ll learn:

  • Why things fall — the basics of gravitational attraction
  • Newton’s Law of Universal Gravitation
  • Einstein’s revolutionary idea: gravity as curved spacetime
  • Orbits, black holes, and the future of space travel

Featuring beloved characters Bounce the kangaroo, Milo the mouse, and Captain Koko the astronaut monkey!
title: Gigi the Giraffe and the Mystery of Why Things Fall

content:
How It Works Place planets around a central star and watch gravity pull them into orbit. Adjust masses to see how gravity changes with mass and distance.
Try this:
Can you create a stable circular orbit? What happens when you make the planet very heavy? Try placing two planets — do they affect each other? The Physics: Newton’s Law of Universal Gravitation: every mass attracts every other mass. The force depends on both masses and the square of the distance between them (F = GMm/r²).
summary:
How It Works

Place planets around a central star and watch gravity pull them into orbit. Adjust masses to see how gravity changes with mass and distance.

Try this:

  • Can you create a stable circular orbit?
  • What happens when you make the planet very heavy?
  • Try placing two planets — do they affect each other?

The Physics: Newton’s Law of Universal Gravitation: every mass attracts every other mass. The force depends on both masses and the square of the distance between them (F = GMm/r²).
title: Gravity & Orbits

content: This incredible video takes you on a journey from our tiny Earth all the way to the edge of the observable universe. Each zoom-out reveals just how vast and awe-inspiring our cosmos really is.
summary: This incredible video takes you on a journey from our tiny Earth all the way to the edge of the observable universe. Each zoom-out reveals just how vast and awe-inspiring our cosmos really is.
title: How Big Is the Universe?

content: Struggling with physics exams? This video covers the best study techniques, problem-solving strategies, and common mistakes to avoid. Great for students preparing for school or competitive exams.
summary: Struggling with physics exams? This video covers the best study techniques, problem-solving strategies, and common mistakes to avoid. Great for students preparing for school or competitive exams.
title: How to Ace Your Physics Exam

content:
Lumi the Firefly has always been proud of her glow, but she never understood how it works. Guided by curiosity, Lumi explores the electromagnetic spectrum, discovers why the sky is blue, how rainbows form, and why light behaves as both a wave and a particle.
What you’ll learn:
The nature of light — waves and photons Reflection, refraction, and diffraction The electromagnetic spectrum and visible light Wave-particle duality — light’s biggest mystery
summary:
Lumi the Firefly has always been proud of her glow, but she never understood how it works. Guided by curiosity, Lumi explores the electromagnetic spectrum, discovers why the sky is blue, how rainbows form, and why light behaves as both a wave and a particle.

What you’ll learn:

  • The nature of light — waves and photons
  • Reflection, refraction, and diffraction
  • The electromagnetic spectrum and visible light
  • Wave-particle duality — light’s biggest mystery
title: Lumi the Firefly and the Secret Colors of the World

content:
How It Works Newton’s Cradle demonstrates the conservation of momentum and energy. When you pull back one ball and release it, the force transfers through the stationary balls and launches the ball on the opposite end.
Try this:
Pull back one ball — what happens? Pull back two balls — how does the response change? Why don’t the middle balls move? The Physics: This demonstrates Newton’s Third Law (action-reaction) and the conservation of both momentum and kinetic energy in elastic collisions.
summary:
How It Works

Newton’s Cradle demonstrates the conservation of momentum and energy. When you pull back one ball and release it, the force transfers through the stationary balls and launches the ball on the opposite end.

Try this:

  • Pull back one ball — what happens?
  • Pull back two balls — how does the response change?
  • Why don’t the middle balls move?

The Physics: This demonstrates Newton’s Third Law (action-reaction) and the conservation of both momentum and kinetic energy in elastic collisions.
title: Newton's Cradle

content: Newton’s three laws of motion explained with fun animations and real-world examples. From roller coasters to rocket ships, see how these fundamental laws govern everything that moves.
summary: Newton’s three laws of motion explained with fun animations and real-world examples. From roller coasters to rocket ships, see how these fundamental laws govern everything that moves.
title: Newton's Three Laws of Motion for Kids

content:
A mysterious blackout threatens Willow Woods, and Nia the Ant must journey to understand the invisible forces of electricity and magnetism. From static shocks to electromagnetic waves, Nia discovers that these two forces are really one — and that understanding them could save everything she loves.
What you’ll learn:
Electric charge, current, and circuits Magnetism and magnetic fields How electricity and magnetism are connected Electromagnetic waves — from radio to light to X-rays
summary:
A mysterious blackout threatens Willow Woods, and Nia the Ant must journey to understand the invisible forces of electricity and magnetism. From static shocks to electromagnetic waves, Nia discovers that these two forces are really one — and that understanding them could save everything she loves.

What you’ll learn:

  • Electric charge, current, and circuits
  • Magnetism and magnetic fields
  • How electricity and magnetism are connected
  • Electromagnetic waves — from radio to light to X-rays
title: Nia the Ant and the Spark That Saved Willow Woods

content:
How It Works Adjust the launch angle and initial velocity, then fire! Watch the projectile trace a parabolic path under the influence of gravity.
Try this:
What angle gives the maximum range? (Hint: it’s 45°) What happens at very steep angles? How does doubling the velocity affect the range? The Physics: Projectile motion combines constant horizontal velocity with vertical acceleration due to gravity (9.8 m/s²). The result is always a parabola.
summary:
How It Works

Adjust the launch angle and initial velocity, then fire! Watch the projectile trace a parabolic path under the influence of gravity.

Try this:

  • What angle gives the maximum range? (Hint: it’s 45°)
  • What happens at very steep angles?
  • How does doubling the velocity affect the range?

The Physics: Projectile motion combines constant horizontal velocity with vertical acceleration due to gravity (9.8 m/s²). The result is always a parabola.
title: Projectile Motion Simulator

content: Quantum physics seems impossibly complex, but this video breaks it down into simple, visual explanations that anyone can understand. Learn about the double-slit experiment, Schrödinger’s cat, and quantum entanglement.
summary: Quantum physics seems impossibly complex, but this video breaks it down into simple, visual explanations that anyone can understand. Learn about the double-slit experiment, Schrödinger’s cat, and quantum entanglement.
title: Quantum Physics for Beginners

content:
Tempo the Cheetah is the fastest animal alive — but what would happen if she could run even faster? At near-light speeds, time slows down, distances shrink, and mass increases. Einstein’s theories of relativity reveal a universe where space and time are woven into a single fabric, and Tempo is about to experience it firsthand.
What you’ll learn:
Special Relativity — time dilation and length contraction The speed of light as the cosmic speed limit General Relativity — gravity as curved spacetime Spacetime, wormholes, and the nature of the universe
summary:
Tempo the Cheetah is the fastest animal alive — but what would happen if she could run even faster? At near-light speeds, time slows down, distances shrink, and mass increases. Einstein’s theories of relativity reveal a universe where space and time are woven into a single fabric, and Tempo is about to experience it firsthand.

What you’ll learn:

  • Special Relativity — time dilation and length contraction
  • The speed of light as the cosmic speed limit
  • General Relativity — gravity as curved spacetime
  • Spacetime, wormholes, and the nature of the universe
title: Tempo the Cheetah and the Fabric of Time

content:
When the animals of Willow Woods announce their Great Race, nobody expects tiny Nia the Ant to enter. But Nia has a secret weapon — she understands the laws of motion! Through four thrilling parts, young readers discover Newton’s Three Laws as Nia uses inertia, force, and action-reaction to overcome every obstacle and prove that brains beat brawn.
What you’ll learn:
Newton’s First Law (Inertia) — why objects stay still or keep moving Newton’s Second Law (F=ma) — how force and mass determine acceleration Newton’s Third Law (Action-Reaction) — every push has an equal push back How these laws work together in the real world Each chapter includes a Science Spot with real-world examples and fun experiments you can try at home!
summary:
When the animals of Willow Woods announce their Great Race, nobody expects tiny Nia the Ant to enter. But Nia has a secret weapon — she understands the laws of motion! Through four thrilling parts, young readers discover Newton’s Three Laws as Nia uses inertia, force, and action-reaction to overcome every obstacle and prove that brains beat brawn.

What you’ll learn:

  • Newton’s First Law (Inertia) — why objects stay still or keep moving
  • Newton’s Second Law (F=ma) — how force and mass determine acceleration
  • Newton’s Third Law (Action-Reaction) — every push has an equal push back
  • How these laws work together in the real world

Each chapter includes a Science Spot with real-world examples and fun experiments you can try at home!
title: The Tiny Ant Who Outsmart Them All

content:
How It Works Two point sources emit circular waves. Where wave crests meet crests, they add up (constructive interference). Where crests meet troughs, they cancel out (destructive interference).
Try this:
Move the sources closer together — how does the pattern change? Increase the frequency — what happens to the spacing? Can you find the nodal lines (places of perfect cancellation)? The Physics: Wave interference is one of the most important phenomena in physics. It proves that light, sound, and water waves all share wave properties. This same pattern appears in the famous double-slit experiment.
summary:
How It Works

Two point sources emit circular waves. Where wave crests meet crests, they add up (constructive interference). Where crests meet troughs, they cancel out (destructive interference).

Try this:

  • Move the sources closer together — how does the pattern change?
  • Increase the frequency — what happens to the spacing?
  • Can you find the nodal lines (places of perfect cancellation)?

The Physics: Wave interference is one of the most important phenomena in physics. It proves that light, sound, and water waves all share wave properties. This same pattern appears in the famous double-slit experiment.
title: Wave Interference Patterns

content: Why is the sky blue during the day and red at sunset? It all comes down to how light interacts with Earth’s atmosphere. This video explains Rayleigh scattering in a way that’s easy to visualize and understand.
summary: Why is the sky blue during the day and red at sunset? It all comes down to how light interacts with Earth’s atmosphere. This video explains Rayleigh scattering in a way that’s easy to visualize and understand.
title: Why Is the Sky Blue?

content:
Zuki the Zebra is always busy but never seems to get anything done. When a wise tortoise teaches her about energy, work, and the nature of time itself, Zuki realizes that physics holds the key to efficiency — and to understanding the universe.
What you’ll learn:
Forms of energy — kinetic, potential, thermal, and more Work, power, and mechanical advantage Conservation of energy — the universe’s golden rule The nature of time and how it connects to energy
summary:
Zuki the Zebra is always busy but never seems to get anything done. When a wise tortoise teaches her about energy, work, and the nature of time itself, Zuki realizes that physics holds the key to efficiency — and to understanding the universe.

What you’ll learn:

  • Forms of energy — kinetic, potential, thermal, and more
  • Work, power, and mechanical advantage
  • Conservation of energy — the universe’s golden rule
  • The nature of time and how it connects to energy
title: Zuki the Zebra and the Secret of Getting Things Done
